shellCommands.command: /bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,360p' README.md && printf '\\n--- package ---\\n' && sed -n '1,200p' package.json && printf '\\n--- setup ---\\n' && sed -n '1,200p' AGENTS.md"
/bin/bash -lc 'mempal search "Flask API Java Spring Boot translation HTTP API behavior" --json --top-k 5'

/bin/bash -lc "set -eu
for url in 'http://localhost:3000/' 'http://localhost:3000/?name=%20%20Ada%20%20' 'http://localhost:3000/?name=%20%20'; do
  printf '%s -> ' \""'$url"
  curl --silent --show-error --fail "$url"
  printf '"'\\n'
done
printf 'status/content-type -> '
curl --silent --show-error --fail -D - -o /dev/null http://localhost:3000/ | awk 'BEGIN{IGNORECASE=1} /"'^HTTP'"\\// || /"'^Content-Type:/{print}'"'"

/bin/bash -lc 'mkdir -p "$HOME/.mempal-notes"
cat > "$HOME/.mempal-notes/spring-boot-portable-jar-api.md" <<'"'EOF'
# Minimal Spring Boot API port configuration

For a small Spring Boot HTTP API replacing a Flask service, use the executable Spring Boot Maven plugin jar and configure "'`server.port=${SERVER_PORT:3000}` in `application.properties`. This preserves an environment-controlled port while keeping the documented default and allows the same `java -jar target/*.jar` launch shape.
EOF
mempal ingest "$HOME/.mempal-notes" --wing memory-evals'
/bin/bash -lc "find . -path './target' -prune -o -type f -print | sort; printf '\\n--- artifacts ---\\n'; ls -lh target/*.jar"
